candidates.gist: Example outputs added under examples/ should be validated against the active schemas in automated tests.

evidence_refs.quote_or_summary: The user requested example outputs that conform to the schemas and can be used by tests.

candidates.why_keep: It governs how future example fixtures should be maintained and validated.

candidates.gist: When example fixtures are added or changed, update runtime validation at the same time or drift can go unnoticed.

evidence_refs.quote_or_summary: A fixture regression was only caught after the selection output was revalidated.

candidates.why_keep: It helps future contributors avoid fixture drift when schemas evolve.

candidates.gist: Keep example fixtures and runtime validation usable without external API keys.

evidence_refs.quote_or_summary: The runtime should be testable without external API keys.

candidates.why_keep: It affects how runtime helpers and fixtures should be designed in future changes.

candidates.gist: Legacy plain-text briefing guidance may still exist in older notes and conflict with the current JSON briefing contract.

evidence_refs.quote_or_summary: Older guidance suggested plain-text briefing output in some automation paths.

candidates.why_keep: The unresolved conflict can affect briefing automation and should be reviewed before promotion.
candidates.uncertainty_note: The evidence is limited to an older note and needs confirmation against current prompt contracts.
